{{Quote|Excellent. Send two more baron droids to kill Shu Mai, and order a dozen to work as my bodyguards. At last we are getting results.|[[Viceroy of the Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation Viceroy]] [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]]|<ref name="NEGD">''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref>}}
A step up from the standard [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]], '''E4 baron droids''' were [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] used by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]].<ref name="SW:OW" /> [[Baktoid Combat Automata/Legends|Baktoid Combat Automata]] developed the experimental baron droid as an all-purpose security unit. The success of the E4 baron led the Trade Federation to commission Baktoid to refine the model for mass production, resulting in the [[B2-series super battle droid/Legends|B2-series super battle droid]].

==Characteristics==
The E4 baron droid were hulking [[Droid/Legends|droids]], capable of impressive feats of strength. Their plating was made of a heavy metal known as [[Arcetron/Legends|Arcetron]]. It could track an opponent with motion, heat, energy, and sonic detectors. Visual stimuli were processed through the droid's single [[Photoreceptor/Legends|photoreceptor]], which could also produce a blinding spotlight to identify and track its targets. They were equipped with two dual [[Repeating blaster cannon/Legends|repeating blaster cannons]] mounted on their [[Limb/Legends|arms]]. They could also function for extended periods without a direct connection to a [[Central Control Computer/Legends|Central Control Computer]].

Though more formidable than their predecessors, they were not without flaws. The baron droid lacked [[Personal energy shield/Legends|personal energy shields]], allowing them to be quickly disabled with multiple hits from a [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]], or a well-aimed [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] slash. In addition, their movement was heavy and slow.

==History==
[[File:Baronmissiledroid.jpg|thumb|left|400px|Another type of "baron droid" in service to the Trade Federation]]
[[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] encountered such [[Droid/Legends|droids]] during [[Gender/Legends|his]] [[Skirmishes on Coruscant|investigation]] of the [[Black Heth]] criminal gang on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. The local [[Naboo (people)/Legends|Naboo]] fighters had been fighting them in swamps and they learnt to avoid them by watching for their signature spotlights.<ref name="NEGD" /> There was another type of [[E4 baron droid|baron battle droid]] that was known by that designation; it too was used by the Trade Federation.<ref name="SW:GB">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''</ref> 

[[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]] planned to use a pair of E4 baron droids as his personal [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] corps, but they were called up for use in the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]] instead. E4s patrolled the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Theed/Legends|Theed]] during the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|invasion]].<ref name="SW:OW" /> Although the [[Trade Federation Droid Army/Legends|Trade Federation Droid Army]] was defeated, the success of the baron droid led to a refinement of the design and the production of the [[B2-series super battle droid/Legends|B2 super battle droids]] as a heavy [[Soldier/Legends|infantry]] unit for the [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|Separatist Droid Army]].<ref name="EGW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>

Many of the droids remained on [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]], where they were eventually hunted down by soldiers of the [[Royal Naboo Security Forces/Legends|Royal Naboo Security Forces]]. Eventually, a squad of baron droids took over the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Arzid]], where they ruled by "the law of the blaster" in [[16 ABY/Legends|16 ABY]].

{{Droid-stub}}

[[File:Baron_droid.jpg|thumb|left|150px|The ''Star Wars: Obi-Wan'' depiction of an E4 baron droid]]

==Behind the scenes==
The baron droids encountered in the [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: Obi-Wan (video game)|''Star Wars: Obi-Wan'']] are noticeably different from the illustration in ''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'', most notably the appearance of the outer plating, as well as the one from the game being bulkier.
